narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after all the effort you've put into your law school dreams, and I want to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to the bar exam as riskier than we'd hope—landing at a 46% chance of not passing. Picture it like a story of your academic journey so far: your Year 3 decile of 2.0 stood out as the biggest chapter pulling toward that tougher outcome, suggesting the demands of upper-level coursework hit harder than for many peers. Layered on that, your LSAT score of 29.3 whispered concerns about the kind of analytical firepower the bar demands, making it the second strongest signal. Early on, your Year 1 decile of 3.0 added to the narrative of steady but not standout performance, while your middle family income bracket hinted at fewer resources to buffer those challenges. Your Native American background, though rich with resilience, factored in as part of patterns we've seen where extra hurdles can weigh on success. Your undergrad GPA of 2.9, being female, and planning full-time studies each nudged the story further toward caution, painting a picture of someone facing an uphill climb without the strongest tailwinds. It's not that you're not capable—far from it—but together, these pieces formed a profile our model couldn't confidently bet on for bar passage. The good news? This is just one snapshot; with targeted boosts like LSAT prep or study strategies, you could rewrite this story entirely. Hang in there—you've got the heart for this.
